steps.title: Find the key claim
steps.content: The passage states that Nunez and colleagues claim the tendency to map time onto a front/back axis is “not in fact ubiquitous.”

B shows that when speaking about the future, Yupno speakers gesture ahead when facing uphill but behind when facing downhill, proving their temporal gestures are not locked to a fixed front/back axis and thus vary with context.
The other three either describe unrelated gesture habits, mention indoor/outdoor frequency, or note a grammatical similarity to Spanish, none of which challenges the universality claim.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
steps.title: Match the finding to the claim
